meta.uncertainty_note: Moderate sample with strong consistency: emphasize repeated architecture, delivery, reliability, and evaluation signals; avoid overfitting around niche frameworks, domains, or formal management because those remain low-frequency in this category.

category_signature.one_sentence_summary: A top-third CV for this category should present a senior IC or light player-coach who ships production AI platforms and features, proves architecture and reliability outcomes, and translates cross-functional needs into scalable applied AI systems rather than pure advanced experimentation or formal people management.
distinctive_signals: Architecture/platform scope is the clearest category anchor: platform design appears in 34/34 roles (100%), with most roles spanning both greenfield and optimization work (28/34).

This category is delivery-heavy and senior-IC-led: senior_ic appears in 34/34 roles, with scope split between IC (19/34) and player-coach (15/34), while hiring (3/34, 8.8%) and performance management (1/34, 2.9%) stay rare.

GenAI emphasis is practical rather than advanced experimentation-led: evaluation/quality is stronger (15/34, 44.1%) than RAG (10/34, 29.4%), agents/orchestration (9/34, 26.5%), prompt engineering (8/34, 23.5%), or fine-tuning (4/34, 11.8%).
citations: Seniority: senior_ic 34/34; role scope IC 19/34, player-coach 15/34.
Management signals: hiring 3/34 (8.8%), org building 8/34 (23.5%), performance management 1/34 (2.9%).
AI stack: evaluation_quality 15/34 (44.1%), RAG 10/34 (29.4%), agents_orchestration 9/34 (26.5%), prompt_engineering 8/34 (23.5%), fine_tuning 4/34 (11.8%).

headline_pattern.recommended_structure: <Staff|Principal> AI Engineer | Applied AI Platform & Architecture | Production GenAI, Evaluation, Reliability
evidence_first_rules: Use a Staff/Principal headline only when the CV already shows senior IC scope; title families cluster around staff_engineer (20/34) and principal_engineer (13/34).
Add an architecture/platform qualifier because platform design is universal in this sample (34/34, 100%).

Add GenAI or applied AI language only if the experience proves production delivery, evaluation, RAG, agents, or guardrails; no single technical keyword exceeds 60%, so keep the headline evidence-backed rather than tool-stuffed.

Do not imply formal people management, hiring ownership, or org leadership in the headline because those signals are limited: hiring 3/34 (8.8%), org building 8/34 (23.5%), performance management 1/34 (2.9%).
citations: Management signals: hiring 3/34 (8.8%), org building 8/34 (23.5%), performance management 1/34 (2.9%).
No hard skill crosses 60%; top hard skill Python is 11/34 (32.4%).

positioning_angle: Position as a hands-on architect for production AI systems who combines platform design, delivery ownership, and reliability evidence.
Show light technical leadership through mentoring, design influence, and stakeholder translation without overstating formal management.
foreground: Production architecture, system integration, reliability, evaluation quality, and scalable delivery across AI-enabled platforms.
Cross-functional execution and communication: collaboration 29/34 (85.3%), communication 23/34 (67.6%), mentoring 20/34 (58.8%).
Applied GenAI patterns when true: evaluation/quality 15/34 (44.1%), RAG 10/34 (29.4%), agents/orchestration 9/34 (26.5%), prompt engineering 8/34 (23.5%).
avoid: advanced experimentation-centric positioning, formal external credential language, or advanced academic-style framing.
Pure people-manager positioning or hiring-led narrative.
Niche stack laundry lists unsupported by outcomes.
safe_positioning: Staff/principal-level AI platform engineer who designs and productionizes scalable AI systems.
Senior IC or player-coach who improves reliability, latency, evaluation quality, and adoption of AI capabilities.
Architect-level engineer who bridges product, platform, and ML delivery in distributed environments.
unsafe_positioning: Formal org owner, people manager, or hiring lead unless explicitly evidenced.
Deep domain specialist in healthcare, fintech, observability, or consulting unless repeated work history proves it.
Cloud- or tooling-specific specialist claims such as deep Azure, Oracle, Grafana, Prometheus, or OpenTelemetry ownership unless explicitly evidenced.
citations: Unsafe claims sample includes formal people-management org ownership, strategic hiring ownership, deep healthcare ownership, deep consulting background, observability tool ownership, and deep oracle cloud specialization.

themes.theme: AI platform architecture and system design
themes.why_it_matters: This category consistently rewards engineers who can design reusable AI-enabled platforms and services, not just model experiments; platform design appears in 34/34 roles (100%).
themes.citation: Platform design 34/34 (100%); valued evidence type architecture 20/20 deep-analysis samples.

themes.why_it_matters: Top profiles show both building new systems and improving existing ones, which matches the dominant delivery pattern in the sample.
The role sits closer to platform and production engineering than to isolated model work, so distributed systems, APIs, microservices, and integration depth can separate stronger CVs.
themes.citation: Distributed systems 4/34 (11.8%), microservices 4/34 (11.8%), APIs 3/34 (8.8%); pain points emphasize robust data flows, serving APIs, large-scale maintainability, and low latency.

themes.why_it_matters: Reliability is repeatedly valued in deep analysis and in success metrics, so CV bullets should prove stable production operation rather than only feature shipment.
themes.citation: Valued evidence type reliability 18/20; success metrics include reliability, resilience, and high availability; monitoring appears in 4/34 (11.8%).

themes.theme: Evaluation and quality measurement for AI systems
themes.why_it_matters: Evaluation is the strongest AI-specific signal in this category and distinguishes production-minded AI engineers from generic GenAI implementers.
themes.citation: Evaluation_quality 15/34 (44.1%); valued evidence type evaluation 15/20 deep-analysis samples.

themes.why_it_matters: Hiring pain points repeatedly mention low latency, high throughput, and maintainable scale, so outcome metrics here carry more weight than generic model claims.
themes.citation: Valued evidence types scale 14/20 and cost 11/20; success metrics include scalability, performance, operational excellence, latency optimization; pain points cite low-latency and high-throughput constraints.

themes.why_it_matters: RAG is meaningful in this category but not universal, so it should strengthen the CV when real delivery evidence exists rather than dominate the narrative by default.
Agentic workflows appear often enough to matter, especially when tied to production controls, reuse, or automation outcomes.

themes.theme: Guardrails, prompting, and safe AI behavior
themes.why_it_matters: These signals support stronger applied AI stories, but they are still weaker than evaluation and platform delivery, so they should not anchor the whole CV unless deeply evidenced.
This role requires translating architecture and delivery choices across product, platform, and engineering stakeholders; collaboration and communication are the most repeated soft-skill signals.
Mentoring is common and supports a player-coach profile, but it should be framed as technical uplift rather than line management.
Some roles include broader org-shaping influence, but the evidence does not support treating formal org ownership as standard for the category.

key_achievement_archetypes.archetype: Built or evolved a production AI platform used by multiple teams or workflows
key_achievement_archetypes.what_it_proves: Shows the category's core value: platform architecture plus real delivery, reuse, and adoption at senior IC scope.
key_achievement_archetypes.citation: Platform design 34/34 (100%); valued evidence types architecture 20/20, delivery 20/20, reliability 18/20, scale 14/20.

key_achievement_archetypes.archetype: Improved AI system quality with evaluation, benchmarking, or feedback loops
key_achievement_archetypes.what_it_proves: Demonstrates mature applied AI engineering and distinguishes the CV from generic GenAI delivery claims.
key_achievement_archetypes.citation: Evaluation_quality 15/34 (44.1%); valued evidence type evaluation 15/20; success metrics include reliability, performance, and operational excellence.

key_achievement_archetypes.archetype: Shipped retrieval or agent workflows with safe operational behavior
key_achievement_archetypes.what_it_proves: Shows practical GenAI experience in the patterns most relevant to this category without drifting into advanced experimentation framing.
key_achievement_archetypes.citation: RAG 10/34 (29.4%), agents_orchestration 9/34 (26.5%), prompt_engineering 8/34 (23.5%), guardrails_governance 5/34 (14.7%), cost evidence 11/20.

key_achievement_archetypes.archetype: Scaled an AI-enabled service under latency, throughput, or reliability constraints
key_achievement_archetypes.what_it_proves: Signals that the candidate can handle the production engineering side of applied AI, which is central to staff/principal hiring in this sample.
key_achievement_archetypes.citation: Success metrics include scalability, performance, resilience, reliability, high availability, and latency optimization; valued evidence types reliability 18/20, scale 14/20, cost 11/20.

key_achievement_archetypes.archetype: Raised team delivery standards through technical leadership and mentoring
key_achievement_archetypes.what_it_proves: Supports a player-coach profile without overclaiming formal management and aligns with the category's strong collaboration and mentoring evidence.
key_achievement_archetypes.citation: Collaboration 29/34 (85.3%), communication 23/34 (67.6%), mentoring 20/34 (58.8%), player-coach scope 15/34, org_building 8/34 (23.5%).

highest_weight_roles: Staff/principal IC roles with platform, architecture, or applied AI delivery scope.
Technical lead or architect roles where the candidate designed production systems and influenced multiple teams.
Senior engineering roles with clear reliability, scale, API, distributed-systems, or MLOps-style outcomes that map into AI platform work.
expand_in_work_history: Roles with direct evidence of platform design, reusable infrastructure, production AI delivery, evaluation, reliability, or cross-team architecture influence.
Work that shows both greenfield building and optimization of existing systems.
Bullets with measurable outcomes in latency, uptime, quality, scalability, or adoption.
compress_in_work_history: Older roles that are primarily feature delivery without architecture scope.
Work centered on narrow tools or domains that do not map to platform, AI delivery, or production reliability.
People-management details unless they are explicit and material, because formal management is not the dominant pattern in this category.
how_to_frame_non_ai_experience: Translate non-AI platform work into transferable signals: distributed systems, APIs, microservices, cloud delivery, CI/CD, observability, reliability, and scale.
If the candidate has strong architecture experience outside AI, tie it forward to productionizing AI systems rather than pretending deep model specialization.
Use prior search, data, adtech, or backend scale work to prove relevance, ranking, routing, throughput, or serving depth when true.
citations: Valued evidence types architecture 20/20, delivery 20/20, reliability 18/20, scale 14/20.
Management signals remain limited: hiring 3/34 (8.8%), performance management 1/34 (2.9%).
Architecture/platform scope 34/34 (100%); management signals remain modest.

avoid_claims: Formal people-management org ownership unless explicitly evidenced; unsafe claim examples include formal people-management org ownership and direct people management or hiring ownership.
Strategic hiring ownership unless explicitly evidenced; hiring appears in only 3/34 roles (8.8%).
Deep healthcare, fintech, observability, consulting, or Oracle-specialist positioning unless repeated work history proves it.
Hands-on ownership of Grafana, Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, or OpenTelemetry in production unless the CV clearly shows it.
Primary ownership of front-end or unrelated stacks such as Ruby on Rails and React unless supported by actual experience.
End-to-end compliance, HIPAA, patient-safety, or responsible-AI program ownership unless explicitly stated.
title_inflation_risks: Avoid upgrading senior engineer experience into staff/principal unless multi-team architecture and delivery scope is visible.
Avoid manager or director titles when the evidence supports senior IC or light player-coach positioning.
Avoid 'AI Research Lead' framing because research-heavy demand is only 1/34 (2.9%).
research_framing_risks: Keep publications, research, and PhD language out of positive positioning unless directly required by the target role.
Do not imply research ownership, model invention, or academic specialization from applied platform work.
domain_or_region_risks: Do not claim native-language requirements; none are evidenced in the deep-analysis summary.
Do not overstate remote-only preference even though remote-first appears often; present distributed collaboration as a working style, not a core identity.
Do not overfit the CV to one vertical because domain distribution is mixed and shallow: fintech 3/34, healthcare 3/34, consulting 2/34, enterprise SaaS 2/34.
citations: Unsafe claims sample includes formal people-management org ownership, strategic hiring ownership, deep healthcare ownership, observability ownership at scale, deep Oracle specialization, deep consulting background, and unsupported stack ownership.
Native language requirements: none. Domain distribution: fintech 3/34, healthcare 3/34, consulting 2/34, enterprise_saas 2/34.

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Anchor the CV around production AI platform architecture and delivery rather than generic GenAI enthusiasm.
support: Platform design 34/34 (100%).
Valued evidence types architecture 20/20 and delivery 20/20.
Title families concentrate in staff_engineer 20/34 and principal_engineer 13/34.

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Use a senior IC or light player-coach narrative, not a formal management narrative.
support: Seniority distribution: senior_ic 34/34.
Role scope: IC 19/34, player-coach 15/34.
Hiring 3/34 (8.8%), performance management 1/34 (2.9%), org_building 8/34 (23.5%).

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Prioritize quantified reliability, evaluation, scale, latency, and cost outcomes in bullet writing.
support: Valued evidence types: reliability 18/20, evaluation 15/20, scale 14/20, cost 11/20.
Success metrics include scalability, performance, resilience, reliability, operational excellence, and latency optimization.
Pain points repeatedly emphasize maintainability, low latency, throughput, and production serving.

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Treat RAG and agents as differentiators, not universal anchors, unless the candidate truly has them.
support: RAG 10/34 (29.4%), agents_orchestration 9/34 (26.5%), prompt_engineering 8/34 (23.5%).
No hard skill crosses 60%; Python is highest at 11/34 (32.4%) in hard_skills_top and 12/34 (35.3%) in language requirements.

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Repeat a small set of true keywords instead of listing many niche tools.
support: ATS strategy should center on strongest repeated concepts because the long-tail frameworks are sparse: PyTorch 6/34 (17.6%), LangChain 4/34 (11.8%), LangGraph 1/34 (2.9%), LlamaIndex 1/34 (2.9%).
No single technical keyword is universal in this category.

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Keep research, publication, and PhD framing inside risk controls only.
support: Research-heavy 1/34 (2.9%).
Requires PhD 1/34 (2.9%).

evidence_ledger.recommendation: State distributed collaboration and mentoring when true, but avoid overclaiming org ownership or regional specialization.
support: Collaboration 29/34 (85.3%), communication 23/34 (67.6%), mentoring 20/34 (58.8%).
Remote-first 11/34, distributed 5/34, hybrid 3/34.
Native language requirements are absent; domain and region-specific signals are weak.

evidence_ledger.recommendation: Use keyword repetition selectively: strongest 4-6 keywords 2-3 mentions each if true; secondary 4-6 keywords 1-2 mentions each if true; niche keywords once only and only if true.
support: Strong repeated category concepts are architecture/platform, reliability, evaluation, collaboration, communication, mentoring, and production AI delivery.
The long-tail stack evidence is fragmented, so over-repetition of niche tools would overfit the CV.
